name: System Topology

architecture_decisions.decision: Use one member-facing web application plus an admin operations surface backed by modular backend domains.
architecture_decisions.rationale: MVP workflows share trip, member, ledger, media, and receipt contexts; separate admin capability is required for moderation and support.

architecture_decisions.decision: Model public contributions through an ExternalContributionProvider boundary instead of Crewly-owned payment processing.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Stage 2 mitigated RISK-003 by delegating checkout, KYC, refunds, disputes, and payouts externally.

architecture_decisions.decision: Run OCR, translation, FX lookup, notifications, and provider reconciliation as asynchronous background work.
architecture_decisions.rationale: These workflows depend on external services, can fail independently, and should not block core trip interactions.

data_systems: object storage for receipts and media

boundaries.name: Public/private visibility boundary
boundaries.description: Public trip content is explicitly selected and never inferred from private trip state.

boundaries.name: External contribution boundary
boundaries.description: Crewly stores provider references and statuses only; money movement remains outside Crewly.

boundaries.name: Ledger scope boundary
boundaries.description: Trip contribution references, trip expenses, and private event expenses remain separate domains.

boundaries.name: Receipt source/display boundary
boundaries.description: Original receipt values are source of truth; OCR, translation, and FX values are derived display data.

risks_and_constraints.risk: External provider selection may limit contribution status import or reconciliation.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Design provider-agnostic references with manual reconciliation fallback.

stage_4_handoff_notes: UX must distinguish public trip page, private trip workspace, and private event workspace.
Contribution CTA should clearly communicate external-provider checkout.
stage_5_handoff_notes: Implement async workers for receipts, notifications, and provider reconciliation.
